item.title: 倔强的普拉博沃与印尼富豪及市场怀疑论者针锋相对
item.description:
该国总统表示，他遭到金融界关键人物的误解，但他们最终会转变态度。【原文：

The nation’s president says he’s misunderstood by key players in the financial world, and they’ll eventually come around.

】

item.title: 澳大利亚外长表示，该国在伊朗战争中的角色仍将保持防御性
item.description: 一位政府高级官员表示，澳大利亚在伊朗日益升级的战争中的参与将仅限于为波斯湾的合作伙伴提供防御支持，但并未承诺协助保护霍尔木兹海峡的航运安全，尽管美国正寻求盟友的协助。【原文：Australia’s involvement in the escalating war in Iran will be limited to providing defensive support for partners in the Persian Gulf, a senior government official said, stopping short of committing to help protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz as the US seeks assistance from allies.】

item.title: 日本财务相片山表示，必要时将在外汇市场采取果断措施
item.description: 日本财务大臣表示，鉴于中东局势持续紧张，日元兑美元汇率正趋近关键关口，当局已做好准备，必要时将采取果断措施应对外汇市场的波动。【原文：Japan’s finance minister said authorities are prepared to respond to movements in the currency market with bold steps if necessary, as the yen weakens toward a key threshold against the dollar amid ongoing Middle East tensions.】

item.title: 特朗普表示，他希望先解决伊朗问题，然后再转向古巴
item.description: 唐纳德·特朗普总统表示，古巴希望与美国达成协议，但他希望先结束伊朗战争，然后再转向这个岛国。【原文：President Donald Trump said Cuba wants to make a deal with the US, but that he wants to finish the war in Iran before turning to the island nation.】

item.title: 西太平洋银行首席执行官米勒表示，能源价格飙升加剧了澳大利亚储备银行面临的挑战
item.description: 西太平洋银行集团首席执行官安东尼·米勒表示，近期油价的剧烈波动使澳大利亚储备银行在应对通胀压力时面临的局面更加复杂。【原文：Westpac Banking Corp. Chief Executive Officer Anthony Miller said the recent oil-price shock is adding to a complicated picture for the Reserve Bank of Australia as it navigates inflationary pressure.】

item.title: 京东进军欧洲，中国零售巨头与亚马逊正面交锋
item.description: 京东集团正进军英国及其他欧洲国家，这是其海外扩张战略的一部分。作为中国版亚马逊，京东在国内市场面临着激烈的竞争。【原文：JD.com Inc. is launching in the UK and other European countries, part of an overseas expansion as China’s answer to Amazon faces intense competition in its home market.】

item.title: 台湾利率互换市场因通胀及汇率风险转向押注加息
item.description: 随着油价飙升和货币贬值引发通胀担忧，固定收益投资者已将未来数月台湾可能加息的预期纳入了定价考量。【原文：Fixed-income investors are pricing in an interest-rate hike in Taiwan in the coming months as surging oil prices and a weakening currency spark inflation concerns.】

item.title: 《金融时报》称，特朗普暗示若未获霍尔木兹海峡援助将推迟与习近平的峰会
item.description: 据《金融时报》援引对美国总统的采访报道，唐纳德·特朗普表示，如果北京方面不协助疏通霍尔木兹海峡，他可能会推迟与国家主席习近平原定的峰会。【原文：Donald Trump said he could delay his planned summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping if Beijing doesn’t help unblock the Strait of Hormuz, the Financial Times reported, citing an interview with the US president.】

item.title: 路透社称，中国愿意增加购买美国农产品
item.description: 据路透社报道，在巴黎举行的双边贸易谈判首日，中方谈判代表表示愿意增购美国农产品——包括禽肉、牛肉和非大豆类大田作物。【原文：Chinese negotiators showed an openness to making additional purchases of US agricultural products — including poultry, beef and non-soybean row crops — during the first day of bilateral trade talks in Paris, Reuters reported.】

item.title: 中国电力“超级电网”为习近平提供了应对能源冲击的缓冲
item.description: 中国长期以来致力于拓展能源供应的努力，正从中东战争中获得新的动力，这进一步巩固了其既定战略——该战略促使电网运营商大举发行债券，并将数千亿美元资金注入市场。【原文：China’s long-running effort to build out its energy sources is getting fresh momentum from the war in the Middle East, reinforcing a strategy that’s sent grid operators on a bond-selling binge and funneled hundreds of billions of dollars into the market.】

item.title: 稀土矿商莱纳斯推进向五角大楼供货的计划
item.description: 莱纳斯稀土有限公司已签署一份具有约束力的意向书，将在未来四年内向五角大楼供应稀土氧化物。【原文：Lynas Rare Earths Ltd. has signed a binding letter of intent to supply the Pentagon with rare-earth oxides over a four year-period.】

item.title: 墨西哥创下“史上最大规模足球课”吉尼斯世界纪录
item.description: 周日，墨西哥在首都的主广场举办了全球规模最大的足球课，该国正为今年6月联合举办世界杯做准备。【原文：Mexico held the world’s largest soccer class on Sunday in the main square of its capital as the country prepares to co-host the World Cup in June.】

item.title: 随着通胀担忧加剧，美国国债2026年收益全数回吐
item.description: 受战争推高的油价飙升影响，投资者对通胀和增长风险感到恐慌，美国国债市场今年以来的所有涨幅已荡然无存。【原文：The US Treasury market has erased all its gains for the year as the war-driven surge in oil prices sets investors panicking about both inflation and growth risks.】

item.title: 英国承诺投入10亿英镑，以新举措应对青年失业危机
item.description: 作为解决青年失业问题的新举措的一部分，英国政府将向雇主提供数千英镑的补贴，以鼓励他们雇佣年轻人。青年失业问题正逐渐演变成基尔·斯塔默首相领导的政府面临的危机。【原文：UK employers are to be offered thousands of pounds to hire young people as part of a new drive to tackle youth joblessness, an issue that is becoming a crisis for Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s government.】

item.title: 金价持稳，油价飙升，中东冲突进入第三周
item.description: 随着中东战争进入第三周，交易员们正评估全球石油供应面临的进一步威胁，金价保持平稳。【原文：Gold held steady as the war in the Middle East entered a third week and traders weighed further threats to global oil supplies.】

item.title: 受哈尔格油田遇袭事件影响，中东局势紧张加剧，油价上涨
item.description: 在美国对伊朗主要出口枢纽发动袭击后，油价一度上涨3.3%。此次袭击标志着这场战争再度升级，过去两周多来，这场战争已几乎切断了全球客户从该地区获取能源供应的渠道。【原文：Oil rose as much as 3.3% after US attacks on Iran’s main export hub marked another escalation in the war that’s all but cut off global customers from the region’s energy supplies for more than two weeks.】

item.title: LVMH旗下风投公司L Catterton计划为日本投资项目筹集3.13亿美元
item.description: 由法国奢侈品集团LVMH酩悦轩尼诗路易威登集团（LVMH Moet Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE）支持的私募股权公司L Catterton计划在未来三年内向五家日本消费品企业投资约500亿日元（3.13亿美元）。【原文：L Catterton, the private equity firm backed by French luxury fashion house LVMH Moet Hennessy Louis Vuitton SE, is planning to invest around ¥50 billion ($313 million) in five Japanese consumer businesses over the next three years.】

item.title: 美国在伊朗关键出口枢纽附近发动空袭后，油价飙升
item.description: 2026年3月15日，伊朗德黑兰，贝里亚纳克区的一栋房屋在两天前的导弹袭击中受损，居民们正在清理房屋内的瓦砾。【原文：Residents clear rubble in a house in the Beryanak District after it was damaged by missile attacks two days before, in Tehran, Iran on March 15, 2026.】

item.title: Triton Partners 为推迟推出的旗舰基金筹集了55亿欧元
item.description: 知情人士透露，Triton Partners 已为其新旗舰基金筹集了 55 亿欧元（63 亿美元）。此前，这家欧洲私募股权公司的募资能力曾因企业文化争议和严峻的募资环境而受挫，此次募资标志着该公司迈出了重要一步。【原文：Triton Partners has raised €5.5 billion ($6.3 billion) for its new flagship fund, people familiar with the matter said, an important milestone after the European buyout firm’s ability to attract fresh money was marred by work culture controversies and a difficult fundraising environment.】

item.title: 军工金属钨表现抢眼，涨幅达557%，远超黄金和铜
item.description: 一种用于制造武器和半导体的金属正成为地缘政治紧张局势的焦点，因为中国的出口限制和不断增长的军事需求导致供应紧张，使其价格飙升至历史新高。【原文：A metal used in weapons and semiconductors is finding itself the subject of geopolitical tensions as Chinese export limits and rising military demand squeeze supplies, sending prices to record highs.】

item.title: 股市和债市开盘前，市场关注原油供应情况
item.description: 在美国对伊朗关键出口枢纽哈尔格岛附近发动空袭，加剧了市场对中东原油供应中断的担忧后，在纽约股市周日晚间恢复交易前，原油市场成为投资者关注的焦点。【原文：Oil dominated investor attention ahead of the reopening of trading Sunday night in New York after US strikes near Iran’s key export hub at Kharg Island heightened fears of disruptions to Middle East crude supplies.】

item.title: 美国重申，因伊朗战争而释放的石油储备将采取互换形式
item.description: 美国能源部周日重申，计划从国家战略石油储备中释放的1.72亿桶原油将采取交换形式进行。【原文：The US Energy Department reiterated Sunday that a planned 172-million-barrel release of oil from the country’s Strategic Petroleum Reserve would be structured as an exchange.】

item.title: 受油价影响的债券市场正考虑将关注重点转向增长担忧
item.description: 债券投资者开始思考，伊朗战争引发的通胀担忧是否很快会演变为对高油价可能威胁经济增长的担忧。【原文：Bond investors are starting to ponder whether the inflation worries sparked by the Iran war will soon tip over into concern about the risk to economic growth from elevated oil prices.】

item.title: 随着伊朗局势加剧通胀，澳大利亚央行将连续两次加息
item.description: 当澳大利亚央行本周召开会议，作出今年第二次利率决议时，央行董事会将发现，受中东战事影响而引发的能源价格冲击波，正使当前的通胀问题进一步恶化。【原文：When Australia’s central bank convenes this week for its second interest-rate decision of the year, the board will find its current inflation problem further exacerbated by an energy price shockwave rippling out from a Middle East driven by war.】

item.title: 受伊朗战争影响，海湾经济体面临自1990年代以来最严重衰退的风险
item.description: 如果伊朗战争不能尽快结束，它可能会对海湾地区最大的经济体——包括沙特阿拉伯、阿联酋和卡塔尔——造成重大打击。【原文：The Iran war threatens to deal significant blows to the Gulf’s biggest economies, including Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates and Qatar, if it doesn’t end soon.】

item.title: 国际能源署称亚洲将立即动用应急储备石油
item.description: 国际能源署表示，随着亚洲买家迫切希望弥补因中东战事中断而造成的石油供应缺口，此次创纪录的库存释放所提供的原油将立即供应至亚洲市场。【原文：The International Energy Agency said oil from a record stockpile release will be made available in Asia immediately as buyers in the region clamor to replace barrels lost to war-related disruptions in the Middle East.】

item.title: 美国能源部长暗示伊朗冲突可能还会持续数周
item.description: 能源部长克里斯·赖特表示，随着美国和以色列试图摧毁伊朗的军事能力，与伊朗的战争可能还会持续数周，而油价和汽油价格也将居高不下。【原文：Energy Secretary Chris Wright signaled the war with Iran may last several more weeks with oil and gasoline prices elevated as the US and Israel seek to destroy Iranian military capabilities.】

item.title: 对冲基金瞄准奇异期权，以把握跨资产类别的巨大波动
item.description: 自伊朗战争爆发以来，受原油价格剧烈波动的带动，机构投资者纷纷转向交易跨市场波动的特殊混合期权。【原文：The wild swings led by oil since the start of the Iran war have institutional investors turning to exotic hybrid options to trade cross-market gyrations.】

item.title: Polymarket的“五分钟投注”抓住了“令人上瘾”的加密货币热潮
item.description: 在过去的十年里，华尔街的交易周期一直在不断缩短。曾经有效期长达数月的期权，如今已与有效期仅为数周，甚至仅有一天期的期权并存。【原文：Over the past decade, Wall Street has steadily shortened the lifespan of its trades. Options that once lasted months now sit alongside ones that last weeks, or even a single day.】

item.title: 瑞银将于4月面临瑞士政府关于资本要求的决定
item.description: 随着瑞士政府在瑞士信贷倒闭后敲定新规，预计将于4月决定要求瑞银集团（UBS Group AG）持有多少额外资本。【原文：The Swiss government is set to decide in April on how much additional capital it wants UBS Group AG to hold, as the administration finalizes new regulations following the collapse of Credit Suisse.】
